title: salix phylicifolia var. angustifolia
intro: Salix phylicifolia var. angustifolia, also known as Willow-leaved Willow or Narrow-leaved Salix, is a variety of the Salix phylicifolia plant, a type of willow tree native to Europe. The bark of this plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides its medicinal properties, making it used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia var. angustifolia include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menopause, arthritis, and other conditions associated with inflammation, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
